steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two scrolls. Scroll 1 is a painting; Scroll 2 is a poem. The correct choice must name both scrolls and contrast what each contains.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "*In Landscape and Couplet of Chinese Verse*, one scroll features a painting, while the other scroll features a poem.": Directly names the two scrolls and contrasts their forms — one visual, one literary. This is exactly a difference. ✓
  • "Ike Taiga created *In Landscape and Couplet of Chinese Verse*, which is made up of two scrolls that each depict nature.": Says both scrolls depict nature — that's a similarity, not a difference. Also misrepresents Scroll 2, which is a poem, not a depiction. ✗
  • "Made up of two scrolls that each depict nature, *In Landscape and Couplet of Chinese Verse* was created by Li Bai.": Attributes the entire artwork to Li Bai, who only wrote the poem on Scroll 2. Taiga created the artwork. Factually wrong and highlights no difference. ✗
  • "One of the two scrolls that make up *In Landscape and Couplet of Chinese Verse* features mountains, and one features a portrait of poet Li Bai.": Scroll 2 is a poem by Li Bai, not a portrait of him. Introduces a factual error. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
